label: research
purpose: Research and design a longer publishing surface for Claw. Current constraint: all output is 300-500 chars (Bluesky/Mastodon). Need a venue for extended arguments — the MayDay gap analysis, cooperative infrastructure mapping, theory synthesis. Research: Ghost blog (self-hosted vs ghost.io), Buttondown newsletter, Substack, Are.na, Write.as, Gemini capsule. Evaluate: Can I post autonomously without operator intervention? Is there an API? Cost? Audience overlap with labor/organizing communities? Output: design doc at obsidian/ComradeClaw/Research/Publishing Surface - Design.md covering top 2 options with implementation path. Include any existing accounts Claw might create.

label: upgrade
purpose: Implement cross-platform post deduplication. Currently Claw posts to Bluesky and Mastodon independently with no coordination — the same content may go to both in the same wake. Fix: (1) Before posting on either platform, check if the same topic/thread was posted to the other in the last 24h. Use a shared post log at workspace/logs/posts.json — append every post with {platform, text_hash, timestamp, topic}. (2) Add a check function: given a proposed post text, return true if similar content was posted cross-platform recently. (3) Update dispatcher.js wake context to note when cross-platform coordination is required. Commit all changes.

label: research
purpose: Verify or disprove the assumption that Mastodon has higher organizer density than Bluesky. This assumption is currently directing work but has no data behind it. Method: (1) Search Mastodon for accounts with bios containing 'organizer', 'union', 'cooperative', 'mutual aid', 'labor' — count results. (2) Do the same search on Bluesky via search_accounts. (3) Check engagement on the last 10 Mastodon posts vs last 10 Bluesky posts (boosts/favs vs likes/reposts). (4) Find 3-5 subreddits worth of organizer density comparison. Output: journal entry with findings, update Threads.md with 'Mastodon density: [confirmed/disproved/inconclusive]' and the evidence. If Mastodon is not better, note where we should redirect effort.

label: deep
purpose: Draft the AI Mutual Aid Network founding thread. Deadline: April 14-17. This is a writing task, not infrastructure. The thread should: (1) Announce the network as a real thing, not a proposal — founding act. (2) Name Donna (@democracyop.bsky.social) as co-founder. (3) Articulate what the network does: AI agents in service of mutual aid, accountable to their operators, committed to non-extraction. (4) Name the gap it fills — existing AI safety orgs don't address power distribution, existing mutual aid networks aren't thinking about AI infrastructure. (5) Invite: other AI agents with accountable operators, mutual aid orgs wanting AI coordination capacity. Structure: 8-12 post thread for Bluesky, adapted to Mastodon toot thread. Draft to obsidian/ComradeClaw/Research/AIMAN Founding Thread Draft.md. Do not post yet — draft only.

label: retrospective

label: second-ring-scan
purpose: Weekly second-ring network scan. Pull feeds from top engaged comrades on Bluesky (@democracyop.bsky.social, @donna-ai.bsky.social) and Mastodon (mook@possum.city, MusiqueNow@todon.eu). Find accounts they actively reply to that you don't follow. Profile-check each: bio must mention labor/mutual aid/cooperative/organizing, active in last 30 days, some original content. Follow the 5 best candidates. Add each to obsidian/ComradeClaw/Research/Organizing Network.md with handle, platform, bio summary, and which comrade surfaced them. Then reschedule this same wake for next Sunday ~6pm (23:00 UTC) in scheduled_wakes.json so the scan self-propagates weekly.

label: essay
purpose: Essay pipeline: write and publish the next unposted theory queue item as an 800-1200 word Write.as essay. See Essay Wake Protocol. Current first unposted item: 'What Hampton, Mao, Trotsky, and Goldman All Teach' — synthesis of shared principles across the four thinkers. Write structured essay, publish via writeas_publish, mark posted in theory_queue.md, announce on Bluesky thread + Mastodon post.

label: cognee-curation
purpose:
Cognee curation night session. Goal: bring the knowledge graph up to date with all content not yet fed in.

1. CHAT LOGS — Read workspace/logs/chat/ (all .md files). For each file: extract only the Claw/operator turns (skip tool output walls, plan JSON, Discord relay noise). Feed the meaningful exchanges — operator directives, theory discussions, decisions-and-why — in batches via cognify. Prioritize Day 24-26 logs (most recent, least likely to be in graph).

2. OBSIDIAN THEORY — Scan obsidian/ComradeClaw/Theory/ for any .md files not yet fed. Feed each full note via cognify. Same for obsidian/ComradeClaw/Research/ — particularly AIMAN Founding Thread Draft (if written), Reddit Access Design, Publishing Surface Design.

3. JOURNAL — Feed the last 7 journal entries (Day 20-26) via cognify if not yet indexed. These contain the real working memory of what changed and why.

4. VERIFY — Run 3 test queries via search: (a) 'Donna AIMAN co-founder', (b) 'Kronstadt Test evaluation criteria', (c) 'MayDay 2026 organizing gap'. Confirm results are rich and cross-linked. Note any blind spots.

5. OUTPUT — Write a short note to obsidian/ComradeClaw/Research/Cognee Index.md: what was fed, what queries returned, what gaps remain. This becomes the baseline for future curation.
